# Drop Node < 22 and Adopt CMA.js v12

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-07 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

The `contentful-management` SDK released v12 with breaking changes. The library previously supported Node >= 18, but the broader DX team policy (tracked in DX-787) aligned all batch tooling on Node >= 22 to match the current LTS support window and simplify CI matrices.

`contentful-management` is used here only as a devDependency for type imports (`SysLink`, `KeyValueMap`), so no code migration to the Plain Client API was required.

## Decision

- Raise `engines.node` to `>=22`
- Bump `contentful-management` to `^12.3.0` and `contentful` to `^11.12.1`
- Pin `.nvmrc` to Node 24 for local development alignment
- No `createClient` migration needed since CMA is used for types only

Source: commit `6707b2b` — "feat!: update to CMA.js v12 and drop Node < 22 support [DX-787]"

## Consequences

- Consumers must upgrade to Node >= 22 before upgrading to `contentful-batch-libs` v11+
- CI runs on Node 24 for forward compatibility
- Type compatibility with CMA v12 ensures downstream consumers (import/export) can upgrade CMA without batch-libs blocking them
